reference_standard: Mycobacterial culture or composite microbiological + clinical reference
population: Patients with clinical suspicion of pulmonary TB

primary_tier.country: Multi-country (8: South Africa, Uganda, Kenya, India, China, Georgia, Belarus, Brazil)
primary_tier.raw_quote: Sensitivities of Xpert Ultra and Xpert were ... 88% and 83%, respectively, across all 462 participants with culture-positive sputum ... Specificities of Xpert Ultra and Xpert for case detection were 96% and 98% (-2.7%, -3.9 to -1.7) overall ... 1753 participants were distributed to the case detection group (n=1439) and the multidrug-resistance risk group (n=314).

sensitivity_tier_added.raw_quote: Among 698 participants included, 211 (30%) were HIV-positive and 336 (48%) had TB. The sensitivity of Ultra was 90.5% (95% CI 86.8-93.4) and specificity was 98.1% (95% CI 96.1-99.2).
The sensitivity and specificity of Xpert Ultra were 45.0% (149/331) and 96.7% (58/60), respectively.
A total of 41 patients were included, of whom 63% (26/41) were diagnosed with PTB. ... The sensitivity and specificity of Ultra were 50% (13/26) and 100% (15/15), respectively.

Of 447 children, 29 (6.5%) were bacteriologically confirmed on induced sputum and 72 (16.1%) were bacteriologically confirmed. With "bacteriologically confirmed on induced sputum" as a reference, the sensitivity and specificity of Xpert Ultra on stool were 58.6% and 88.1%, respectively.

_engine_expected_flags.note: These are documentation only — the engine recomputes at page-load
_engine_expected_flags.expected_fallback_combined: fe_bivariate (k=5 — REML may converge or fall back depending on Nelder-Mead behaviour on small heterogeneous data)
